name: Dorchester City Park
description: Dorchester City Park is currently the place to play pickleball in Lincoln City, Oregon. There are 8 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent and you may bring your own nets. The Lincoln City Pickleball Club (LCPBC) actively uses these courts and has about 200 members. Open play is from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM Monday thru Friday. LCPB is very welcoming to the pickleball community at large, so feel free to drop in for no fee. There is a donation box, but it’s totally discretionary. LCPBC provides nets and balls during open play. Annual membership to LCPBC is very affordable at $30.00 per year and members are afforded access to the Team Reach smart phone app.
fees: Fee required — Annual club dues are $30 per year.

name: Frank V Wade Memorial Park
description: Additional information regarding play, contact the Newport Pickleball Club at Newportpickleballclub.or@gmail.com or the Newport Pickleball Club Facebook page.

name: Lincoln City Rec Center
description: Lincoln City Rec Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Lincoln City, OR. There are 10 courts. 2 are indoors and 8 are outdoors. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A membership is required to play.

name: Little Whale Cove Home Owners
description: Come play pickleball at Little Whale Cove Home Owners in Depoe Bay, OR! There are 2 outdoor courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. The courts are private.

name: Newport Recreation Center
description: Newport Recreation Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Newport, OR. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free.

name: Yachats Commons
description: Come play pickleball at Yachats Commons in Yachats, OR! There is 1 indoor wood court. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. The court is free.
